jerry-jones.name: Jerry Jones
drama_notes: Bought the Cowboys for $140M in 1989; team is now valued at $11B+ — most valuable franchise in world sports.
30 years without a Super Bowl appearance. The longest active drought of any 'big-market' franchise.
Acts as his own GM. Insists on calling the shots himself. His sons Stephen and Jerry Jr. are in the front office.
His private box at AT&T Stadium has paparazzi access — every Cowboys win/loss is a Jones family reaction shot.
The 'Jerry's World' phrase covers the stadium, the brand, and the dysfunction.
headlines: Owner of the most-valuable sports team in the world.
Pro Football Hall of Fame contributor (2017).
memes: Jerry's eternal 'this is our year' confidence in August.
His sideline access — running onto the field after wins.
'Jerry Jones lecturing the media for 25 minutes' is a weekly content drop.
The 'Stephen Jones rolling his eyes at his dad' photo collection.
off_field: From Los Angeles, CA — University of Arkansas alum (national champion).
Made his fortune in oil + gas before buying the Cowboys.
Wife Gene since 1963. Three kids in the business.
Sister-of-the-month bourbon brand — yes, that's a thing.
running_jokes: Jerry will hire any coach not named Sean Payton.
He'll never sell. He'll never step down. He's 81 and has plans for 91.
Every Cowboys win is 'we're back' season.

robert-kraft.name: Robert Kraft
drama_notes: Bought the Patriots for $172M in 1994; team is now $7B+.
Hired Bill Belichick in 2000, drafted Tom Brady in 2000 — won 6 Super Bowls together.
His 2019 Florida massage parlor solicitation case — charges were dropped after legal battle but the story haunted the franchise.
Brutal divorce from Belichick in 2024 — they reportedly stopped speaking years before the official split.
Friend of Trump, Kraft + Mahomes/Mac Jones era is the 'rebuild from scratch' chapter.
headlines: 6x Super Bowl champion owner — most ever.
Married actress Dana Blumberg in 2024 — the wedding was a celebrity event.
memes: His dance moves at the Travis Scott concert (2019) lived forever online.
His Pats jacket + jeans uniform on the sideline.
The 'sad Kraft' photos after every Patriots loss in the post-Brady era.
off_field: From Brookline, MA — Columbia alum + Harvard MBA.
Made his fortune in paper + packaging (International Forest Products).
First wife Myra (passed 2011) was a beloved figure — community foundation in her name.
Married Dana Blumberg (40 years his junior) in October 2024.
running_jokes: Kraft hugging Tom Brady is required at any major event.
He'll outlive every owner on this list.
Patriots dynasty is over but Kraft isn't going anywhere.

mark-cuban.name: Mark Cuban
drama_notes: Sold majority stake in the Mavs to the Adelson family (Sands casino) in late 2023 — kept basketball operations control.
The sale was a bet on Texas legalizing casino gambling — Adelsons want to build a casino + arena complex in Dallas.
His 2006 Finals loss to the Heat (Wade flopping era) is still his worst sports memory.
Won 2011 NBA title with Dirk Nowitzki — vindication.
Famously confrontational on Twitter — feuded with refs, players, and other owners.
headlines: Sold majority stake in Mavs to Adelson family for $3.5B (2023).
Founded Cost Plus Drugs — disrupting the pharmacy industry.
memes: Sideline Mark Cuban yelling at refs is canon.
His Shark Tank persona being the 'one who'll actually invest.'
The 2024 Luka trade he reportedly didn't see coming.
off_field: From Pittsburgh, PA — Indiana University alum.
Made fortune selling Broadcast.com to Yahoo for $5.7B in 1999.
Wife Tiffany since 2002, three kids.
Departed Shark Tank in 2024.
running_jokes: He'll fine himself $250K for criticizing a ref.
Cost Plus Drugs is the only thing he'll talk about now.
The Adelson era is the franchise's biggest pivot since Dirk.

joe-lacob.name: Joe Lacob
drama_notes: Bought the Warriors with Peter Guber in 2010 for $450M — team is now worth $8B+.
His infamous 2016 NYT interview where he said the Warriors were 'light years ahead' of every other franchise — it aged terribly when KD left and they collapsed.
His son Kirk Lacob was promoted to assistant GM — the nepotism story.
Famously hands-on owner — sits courtside at every home game.
Steve Kerr's contract extensions, Klay's departure, the dynasty's twilight — all happen under his watch.
headlines: Built Chase Center (San Francisco arena, opened 2019).
memes: His courtside reactions during playoff games.
The 'Lacob built this' vs. 'Lacob inherited Steph' debate.
off_field: From New Bedford, MA — UC Irvine alum + Stanford MBA.
Partner at Kleiner Perkins (top VC firm) for 30+ years.
Wife Nicole Curran — they've had public spats with celebrity wives at games (2019 Beyoncé incident).
Got booed at Oracle Arena while presenting Steph's MVP trophy (2017) — said it was 'humbling.'
running_jokes: He'll make you regret every Twitter comment.
Chase Center sushi is somehow always the headline.
The dynasty's afterglow is his entire 2020s storyline.

pat-riley.name: Pat Riley
drama_notes: Architect of the 2010-2014 LeBron/Wade/Bosh super-team — the slide deck that landed LeBron is part of NBA folklore.
9x NBA champion (5 as HC, 4 as exec) — only person with rings as player, coach, and executive.
Rumored to be in his final years with the Heat — 80 years old, still calling the shots.
His feuds with Stan Van Gundy + Avery Johnson are NBA lore.
The 'Pat Riley pinky ring' culture — players who stay in Miami long enough get one (figuratively).
headlines: Founded the 'Heat Culture' phrase that's now copied by every franchise.
Showtime Lakers HC (1980s) — won 4 titles with Magic.
memes: The 'in private I'd been concerned about LeBron' presser when LeBron left in 2014.
His Armani-suit press conferences are an aesthetic.
off_field: From Schenectady, NY — Kentucky alum (1966 NCAA finalist).
Wife Chris since 1970 — long-time advocate for the Heat community.
Has lived in Miami since 1995 — fully a Miami person now.
Famously private, only does interviews on his terms.
running_jokes: He'll outwork every front office in the league at 80.
Pat will call your agent at midnight and have a meeting by morning.
The Heat's culture IS Pat Riley.
